mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances; signal loses predictive power when unemployment growth exceeds 1.0pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive forward returns in >60% of cases; interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect improving factory utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle expansions; 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO changes in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive inflation surprises coincide with positive forward returns in >50% of cases; inflation signal is overwhelmed by liquidity injections.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved consumer spending forecasts.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when payroll growth is driven by temporary government hiring; interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to translate to positive 5-day returns in >65% of observations; earnings revisions lag GDP data by more than one quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained contractions in industrial production signal supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive despite prolonged production declines; signal is neutralized by fiscal stimulus or monetary accommodation.
